rulings.Case Title: EASTERN PLUMAS HEALTHCARE DISTRICT, A POLITICAL SU et al VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH CARE SERVICES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motionto Stay This Case Pending Resolution Of An Earlier-Filed Federal Case Raising Identical Claims And Memerandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 22, 2014, Line 1, RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH CARE SERVICES, TOBY DOUGLAS Motion to Stay This Case Pending Resolution Of An Earlier-Filed Federal Case Raising Identical Claims And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof. The motion for stay is granted. The federal and state complaints raise the same issue, i.e., whether AB 97 (which reduces the Medi-Cal payment rates to petitioners) violates various portions of 42 USC 1396a. The parties in the federal and state actions are the same/substantially similar. Petitioners concede that they are members of the federal plaintiff (CHA). There is clearly a chance for an unseemly conflict. The other factors highlighted in Caiafa Prof. Law Corp. v. State Farm Fire & Cas. Co., (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 800, also weigh in respondents' favor. The federal court is in the best position to apply federal law. The federal action has already been heavily litigated and is hopefully close to resolution. The stay will not prejudice petitioners. First, it is dubious whether petitioners can recover retroactive monetary relief in state court based on principles of sovereign immunity. See Alden v. Maine (1999) 527 U.S. 706, 712. Second, respondent represents that it does not plan to seek reimbursement for at least 6 months. (Gassman dec. par. 6). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE BERNARDO VS. BRUCE MARCUCCI DDS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motoin To Strike Or Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 22, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT BRUCE MARCUCCI Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs. Granted. The memorandum of costs, filed on September 19, 2014, is stricken in its entirety as untimely. Cal. Rule of Court 3.1700(a)(1) provides that a "prevailing party who claims costs must serve and file a memorandum of costs within 15 days after the date of mailing of the notice of entry of judgment." The notice of entry of judgment was mailed on August 26, 2014. The memorandum of costs was filed (and served on the wrong address) on September 19, 2014, beyond the 15 day period. Although Cal. Rule of Court 3.1700(b)(3) permits the Court to extend the times for serving the memorandum of costs, Plaintiff provides no explanation as to why the memorandum of costs was not timely filed, and therefore, the Court declines to extend the time. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: DIANA ANDERSON VS. BRENNER, VICTORIA, AKA VICTORIA MARIE GAIST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion Terminating Sanctions Against Deft Victoria Brenner, And Monetary Sanctions Against Her And Her Attorneys Christina Kim And Gilsleider, Mcmahon & Hermanson For Misuse Of The Discovery Process
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, December 22, 2014, Line 1 (Page 1 of 2): Plaintiff DIANA ANDERSON'S Motion Terminating Sanctions Against Defendant Victoria Brenner, And Monetary Sanctions Against Her And Her Attorneys Christina Kim And Gilsleider, McMahon & Hermanson For Misuse Of The Discovery Process. Page 1 of 2: Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. Continued to Page 2 of 2. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DIANA ANDERSON VS. BRENNER, VICTORIA, AKA VICTORIA MARIE GAIST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Diana Anderson'S Motion Terminating Sanctions Against Defendant Victoria Brenner, And Monetary Sanctions Against Her And Her Attorneys Christina Kim And Gilsleider, Mcmahon & Hermanson For Misuse Of The Discovery Process
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, December 22, 2014, Line 1 (Page 2 of 2): Plaintiff Diana Anderson's Motion Terminating Sanctions Against Defendant Victoria Brenner, And Monetary Sanctions Against Her And Her Attorneys Christina Kim And Gilsleider, McMahon & Hermanson For Misuse Of The Discovery Process. Page 2 of 2: The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion for terminating sanctions against Defendant and for monetary sanctions against Defendant and her counsel is granted-in-part and denied-in-part. The Judge Pro Tem finds that terminating sanctions are not warranted here. Terminating sanctions may only be granted in "extreme situations, such as when the conduct was clear and deliberate." Del Junco, Jr. v. Hugnagel, 150 Cal. App. 4th 789, 799 (2007); see also See R.S. Creative, Inc. v. Creative Cotton, Ltd., 75 Cal. App. 4th 486, 496 (1999) ("[T]erminating sanctions are to be used sparingly, only when the trial court concludes that lesser sanctions would not bring about the compliance of the offending party. The record demonstrates repeated violations of stipulations and court orders, a forged document offered as true, and deliberate destruction of evidence pertinent to exposing that fact."). This is not such an "extreme" situation. First, it is not clear that Defendant violated the Court's June 20, 2014 Order, which was a sequencing order. Second, though it is clear that Defendant violated the Court's July 30, 2014 Order by failing to "sign, verify, and further respond" to form interrogatories and requests for admission within 15 days, it appears that Defense counsel had difficulties communicating with her client and did eventually provide further, verified responses. In addition, it appears that there were significant failures by both parties' counsel to communicate effectively or regularly about these issues. Nonetheless, given Defendant's failure to comply with the July 3, 2014 Order, monetary sanctions are appropriate in lieu of terminating sanctions. See CCP §§ 2030.300(e), 2033.2909(e). Accordingly, monetary sanctions are imposed against Defendant in the amount of $5,100. The Judge Pro Tem does not award additional sanctions related to the denial of Defendant's motion to compel on December 3, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RAYNA BRAND VS. CONSTANCE BAKER-COHN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltff'S Responses To Defts' Discovery Requests; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, December 22, 2014, Line 2: Defendants CONSTANCE BAKER-COHN and DAVID COHN'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Responses To Defendants' Discovery Requests; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' unopposed motion to compel is granted. Plaintiff shall serve written, verified responses (without objections other than privilege) to Defendants' Form Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents and produce responsive documents within 10 days from the date of the hearing. Defendants' request for monetary sanctions is granted. Defendants are awarded sanctions against Plaintiff in the amount of $520, which shall be paid to Defendants' counsel within 30 days of the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STANLEY CILETTI et al VS. DORAN CONSTRUCTION AND DESIGN, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Strike Of Doran Construction And Design, Inc. To Pliantiffs' First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 22, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT DORAN CONSTRUCTION AND DESIGN, INC.'S Motion To Strike Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint. The motion to strike is granted in its entirety without leave to amend. Plaintiff cannot recover general/emotional distress damages as a matter of law. See Erlich v. Menezes (1999) 21 Cal.4th 543, 554; Lubner v. City of Los Angeles (1996) 45 Cal.App.4th 525. The court strikes the allegations regarding defendant's alleged lie. That is an evidentiary fact that is unnecessary and irrelevant to pleading the negligence claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BINH TRAN ET AL VS. LAMBDA PHI EPSILON NATIONAL FRATERNITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 22, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT LAMBDA PHI EPSILON NATIONAL FRATERNITY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is denied. Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to support the claim for punitive damages. Plaintiff can seek punitives on the survivor claims, causes of action 2 and 3. Nothing in Penal Code sec. 245.6 or the Bane Act precludes the recovery of punitive damages. Plaintiff can seek attorney's fees per Civil Code sec. 52.1(h).
